title: Fever and neck stiffness in a 19-year-old
presentation: A 19-year-old male college student presents with 12 hours of worsening headache, fever, and neck stiffness. His roommate brought him in because he was confused this morning. He has a non-blanching petechial rash on his trunk and legs. Vitals: BP 96/58, HR 128, RR 22, Temp 39.8C, SpO2 98%. He is photophobic and cannot touch his chin to his chest.

immediate: IV antibiotics BEFORE LP if delayed
gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Non-blanching rash + meningismus + hemodynamic instability = meningococcemia, do NOT delay antibiotics for LP
